channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination: Here are the breadcrumbs left behind by a killer
item.description: The manhunt for UnitedHealth CEO Brian Thompson's assassin has entered its fourth day. Here is the evidence investigators have amassed thus far.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach the capital's suburbs. Worried residents flee and stock up on supplies
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Insurgents' stunning march across Syria gained speed on Saturday with news that they had reached the suburbs of the capital and with the government forced to deny rumors that President Bashar Assad had fled the country.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An archbishop's knock formally restores Notre Dame to life as winds howl and heads of state look on
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Howling winds couldn’t stop Notre Dame Cathedral ’s heart from beating again. With three resounding knocks on its doors by Paris Archbishop Laurent Ulrich, wielding a specially designed crosier carved from fire-scorched beams, the monument roared back to life Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KREX 5

item.title: La Scala's gala premiere is a Verdi opera with a reputation for bringing bad luck
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Milan’s storied Teatro alla Scala presents Giuseppe Verdi’s “La Forza del Destino” for its gala season premiere Saturday for the first time in 59 years, seeking to shake the opera's reputation for bringing bad luck.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff’s Office between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How 'Mufasa' rose with Aaron Pierre and Blue Ivy's voices along with new Lin-Manuel Miranda music
item.description: When Aaron Pierre was cast as Mufasa, the weight of following in the late James Earl Jones’ legendary footsteps was enough to rattle any actor.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr., Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill would require liquor stores to open the bottle and reseal it upon a sale to curb the secondary resell market of high-end spirits.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deep dive into UnitedHealthcare CEO's personal life will play key role in investigation: retired NYPD officer
item.description: Ralph Cilento, a retired lieutenant commander at the NYPD and adjunct professor of police science at John Jay College of Criminal Justice, spoke to Fox News Digital Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio State University fraternity suspended on hazing allegations
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) – An Ohio State University fraternity has been suspended on allegations of hazing. According to a release by a university spokesperson, the Dean of Students in the Office of Student Life has issued an interim suspension of Alpha Tau Omega based on allegations of hazing.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson remained elusive Friday, even as investigators learned more about his movements before and after the shocking crime. Investigators still don't know his name. They haven't found the gun.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, Fox 5

item.title: MAP: Flu season is here, especially in these states
item.description: Flu activity spiked in states across the U.S. during the last week of November, according to a new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officials release bodycam video of police shooting that killed 15-year-old Ohio boy
item.description: AKRON, Ohio (AP) — The mayor and police chief in an Ohio city where a 15-year-old boy was fatally shot by police on Thanksgiving night said bodycam video of the shooting raises questions that must be addressed. The boy's family said Friday that they want answers, too.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: JetBlue nixing routes between multiple major cities, reducing several others
item.description: Passengers relying on JetBlue routes between certain American cities may need to make alternate travel plans in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Millions will lose health coverage if ACA subsidies expire: CBO
item.description: If Affordable Care Act subsidies are allowed to expire at the end of 2025 millions of people will lose their insurance and premiums will jump, according to a Congressional Budget Office report.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen killed, naked man arrested at California High School
item.description: A 24-year-old man, possibly naked and wielding an ax, was arrested in connection with the killing of a teenager in Newbury Park on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial urged jurors on Friday to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn't reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man in custody after locking himself inside Montgomery County church, deputies say
item.description: Deputies responded to a church in Montgomery County early Friday morning after a man walked into the building and locked himself inside.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
